profile.name: Yusuf İhsan Görgel
profile.headline: I’m a software engineer working across Flutter, Dart, Go, and production infrastructure.
profile.summary: Since 2021, I have built and maintained software for mobile devices, tablets, desktop operating systems, and the web. My work includes ERP and point-of-sale products, logistics workflows, digital publishing, backend services, and the release systems around them.
profile.background: I started with Flutter client applications and grew into the parts around them: native integrations, offline data, real-time synchronisation, Go services, queues, databases, deployment, and production reliability. I still work close to the product and follow features through testing, release, and long-term maintenance.

experience.summary: Project-based since March 2021 and full-time since September 2025. I build and maintain production ERP and point-of-sale applications across Android and iOS tablets plus Linux, macOS, and Windows desktops, with a focus on offline data, native integrations, performance, release reliability, and long-lived product maintenance.
Delivered mobile and web product work spanning Flutter and React, REST and WebSocket data flows, live vehicle tracking, Google and Apple Maps integrations, deployment, and QA coordination.
Contributed to Flutter application delivery and maintenance in a long-running part-time role, carrying product work from implementation through release support.
Shipped the Aydınlık E-Gazete and Bilim ve Ütopya E-Dergi applications for Android and iOS, working across Flutter delivery, API integration, localisation, and store-ready release workflows.
Completed a software engineering internship focused on production development practices, collaborative delivery, and applying academic foundations inside a working engineering team.
Formal study in algorithms, data structures, databases, software design, and team-based product development.

contributions.title: Separate server feature registration from legacy initialization
contributions.problem: Feature registration was coupled to the legacy initialization handshake, making the server lifecycle harder to reuse across transports and initialization modes.
contributions.change: Separate reusable feature and capability registration from legacy protocol negotiation, migrate the mixins and examples, and preserve compatibility with the existing initialization path through regression coverage.

contributions.title: Make Firebase core loading deterministic on WebKit
contributions.problem: Component bundles could execute before firebase-app.js was available, producing an initialization race in WebKit browsers.
contributions.change: Load the Firebase core script before component bundles and preserve deterministic dependency order.

contributions.title: Reset unknown dropdown initial values on first build
contributions.problem: A dropdown could begin with a value that did not exist in its items and fail before normal field reset behaviour ran.
contributions.change: Normalize the first build and cover the regression with widget tests.

contributions.title: Treat SQLite TRUE and 1 defaults as the same schema
contributions.problem: Schema verification reported equivalent SQLite boolean defaults as a migration difference.
contributions.change: Normalize the verifier comparison and add regression coverage for both representations.

contributions.title: Add a Fiber and Asynq background-jobs recipe
contributions.problem: The recipe collection lacked a production-shaped example for enqueueing and processing Redis-backed work outside request handlers.
contributions.change: Add a runnable producer, worker, queue configuration, and usage documentation.

contributions.title: Wait for web rendering before the first-frame event
contributions.problem: On SkWasm, flutter-first-frame can fire before the compositor presents visible Flutter content, leaving a measurable blank handoff window.
contributions.change: Wait for outstanding scene renders and the next browser frame before dispatching the event; the pull request is ready for review with seven Chrome renderer and compiler suites passing.

contributions.title: Ignore unrecognized SVG font-weight values
contributions.problem: SVG text parsing could reject documents when a font-weight value was valid CSS syntax but unsupported by the vector graphics parser.
contributions.change: Treat unrecognized font-weight values as unspecified, preserve supported mappings, and cover the parser behaviour with the existing vector-graphics test suite.

contributions.title: Add SEP-2575 request metadata and discovery types
contributions.problem: The SDK needed a wire model for request-scoped protocol and client context without breaking legacy request shapes.
contributions.change: Add typed experimental metadata accessors, server discovery types, polymorphic codecs, and malformed-input coverage.

systems.name: FugaSoft
systems.summary: The ERP and point-of-sale product family I continue to build at Uzman Adres for retail teams working across tablets and desktop operating systems.
systems.ownership: I work primarily on the Flutter clients and the production concerns around offline data, native integrations, synchronisation, performance, release, and long-term maintenance.
systems.decision: Treat local persistence, device integration, and platform behaviour as product architecture rather than client-side afterthoughts.
systems.challenge: Retail software has to remain fast and dependable across store hardware, intermittent connectivity, long-running sessions, and several operating systems.
systems.approach: Build one shared Flutter product surface while making local data, synchronisation boundaries, native device APIs, and release behaviour explicit for every platform.
systems.outcome: Ongoing production delivery for FugaSoft's ERP and point-of-sale platform across Android and iOS tablets plus Linux, macOS, and Windows desktops.

artifact.label: CURRENT PRODUCT
artifact.alt: FugaSoft's current public website presenting its point-of-sale and business management platform.
artifact.caption: Current FugaSoft product website · captured from the public company site.

systems.name: Dorse
systems.summary: A public logistics platform connecting drivers, fleet companies, and weighbridge businesses through mobile and web workflows.
systems.ownership: I developed the Flutter application and React web surface across live vehicle state, maps, REST and WebSocket flows, deployment, and QA coordination.
systems.decision: Keep real-time vehicle and weighbridge state explicit from transport to interface instead of hiding it behind screen-specific workarounds.
systems.challenge: Drivers, fleet companies, and weighbridge businesses needed to act on the same changing logistics state across mobile and web.
systems.approach: Build Flutter and React clients around shared REST and WebSocket flows, map integrations, offline behaviour, and coordinated release testing.
systems.outcome: Delivered the public mobile and web product for driver, fleet, and weighbridge workflows during the verified April 2024 to January 2025 project period.

artifact.label: PUBLIC PRODUCT
artifact.alt: Dorse's current public product website showing the logistics mobile application in use.
artifact.caption: Dorse product surface · captured from the public company website.

systems.summary: A newspaper client for iPhone and iPad with issue browsing, subscriber archive access, API-backed content, and mobile releases.
systems.ownership: At Promob TR, I worked on the Flutter client, API-backed issue flow, localisation, and mobile releases.
systems.decision: Keep issue browsing, reading, and subscriber archive access inside one legible phone-and-tablet flow.

systems.summary: A magazine client for iPhone and iPad that presents issue, archive, and account controls in one Flutter application.

systems.name: Galvapedia
systems.spotlight: Galvanizing calculators and industrial reference workflows across a released mobile product.
systems.summary: A public industrial reference application with galvanizing calculators and technical workflows for field use.
systems.ownership: I built the product with Flutter and a Node.js, Express, and MongoDB service layer.
systems.decision: Turn specialist coating calculations into focused mobile tools with inputs and results that remain understandable in the field.

artifact.label: PUBLIC APP STORE RELEASE

systems.name: Queue Inspector MCP
systems.spotlight: Typed inspection and guarded recovery controls for Redis-backed job queues.
systems.summary: An MCP server for inspecting and operating Redis-backed Asynq and BullMQ queues without reaching into storage by hand.
systems.ownership: I designed the command surface, typed validation, queue adapters, and conservative state-changing operations.
systems.decision: Keep observation read-only by default; make retries and destructive operations explicit and narrowly scoped.

artifact.label: REPOSITORY ARTIFACT
artifact.alt: Queue Inspector MCP repository cover illustrating guarded inspection and recovery of Redis-backed job queues.
artifact.caption: Published repository cover · queue inspection and guarded recovery.

systems.name: Multi-tenant Gateway
systems.spotlight: Explicit tenant resolution, authentication, policy, and storage boundaries in one Go binary.
systems.summary: A single-binary gateway demonstrating tenant resolution, JWT authentication, per-tenant rate limits, and module boundaries.
systems.ownership: I designed and implemented the reference from transport and tenant resolution through policy, persistence, and test coverage.
systems.decision: Carry tenant context through the request path instead of hiding it behind global state.

artifact.label: ARCHITECTURE RECORD
artifact.alt: Source-derived architecture record for the Go multi-tenant gateway and its explicit request policy chain.
artifact.caption: Architecture and source path drawn from the public repository.

systems.name: Redis Task Queue
systems.spotlight: Retries, dead-letter handling, and weighted queues behind a small Dart API.
systems.summary: A Redis-backed task queue for Dart with retries, dead-letter handling, and weighted queues.
systems.ownership: I designed the public API, queue semantics, failure handling, and runnable examples.
systems.decision: Make failure states inspectable and scheduling behaviour deterministic without adding a runtime framework.

artifact.label: REPOSITORY ARTIFACT
artifact.alt: Redis Task Queue repository cover showing API enqueue, queue, worker, success, and dead-letter paths.
artifact.caption: Published repository cover · queue lifecycle.

systems.name: Constellation Particles
systems.spotlight: A dependency-free interactive particle field with spatial-grid neighbour lookup.
systems.summary: A dependency-free particle field for Flutter with pointer interaction and spatial-grid neighbour lookup.
systems.ownership: I implemented the painter, pointer interaction, spatial partitioning, and package examples without runtime dependencies.
systems.decision: Use spatial partitioning to avoid comparing every particle pair as the scene grows.

artifact.label: RECORDED DEMO
artifact.alt: Constellation Particles demo frame with pointer-reactive particles and local neighbour links.
artifact.caption: Frame from the published Flutter CustomPainter demo.

systems.name: Flutter Web Portfolio
systems.spotlight: A content-driven Flutter Web document with a custom narrative runtime and browser regression suite.
systems.summary: This site is the working record of my experience, shipped products, and open-source contributions.
systems.ownership: I built and run the Flutter Web site, its external content pipeline, accessibility layer, browser regression suite, and production release.
systems.decision: Treat the portfolio as an accessible document first and keep ambient rendering outside its semantic path.
